negativeInsights.finding: Customers buy 'wasp trap' but get primarily a mosquito and fly killer — UV light doesn't attract wasps.
negativeInsights.implication: Listings must either scientifically prove wasp efficacy (CO2 lure, pheromone) or honestly reposition to mosquitoes/flies — otherwise expectation gaps dominate the reviews.

negativeInsights.finding: Bee, bumblebee and butterfly deaths drive a significant share of 1-star reviews — especially among garden and beekeeper customers.
negativeInsights.implication: Highlight selectivity (e.g. baits that attract only wasps, or selective grid spacing) as a USP. Otherwise drop the beekeeper segment entirely from targeting and focus messaging on 'indoor'.

negativeInsights.finding: Crackling/zapping sound on insect death and fan hum are disturbing especially in the bedroom and during BBQs.
negativeInsights.implication: Position a vacuum variant or sticky-paper version instead of high voltage as 'bedroom-suitable'. State noise in dB explicitly in the listing.

negativeInsights.finding: Bright UV light is disturbing at night and dazzles neighbours on the patio — top-3 non-efficacy complaint.
negativeInsights.implication: Dimmer, timer or directional reflector are clear differentiating features. At minimum, state 'light intensity' with lux value in the listing.

negativeInsights.finding: Catch tray hard to remove, cleaning brush missing or unusable — recurring theme.
negativeInsights.implication: 'Tool-free cleaning' and dishwasher-safe tray are low-cost features with high review impact. Highlight in bullet #2 or #3.

negativeInsights.finding: Outdoor promise often not kept in rain — devices fail or get permanently damaged.
negativeInsights.implication: Clear IP-class communication (IP44, IP54) plus 'even in continuous rain' as A+ image. Don't target outdoor use for indoor models.
